creator: kai@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to bitbucket63-it from comment #0)

Thanks for your info. I can confirm it myself on a system that runs FreeBSD 12.4-RELEASE amd64 and that uses a nvidia-driver-390.

It seems to affect only systems that use a nvidia driver, because Anki runs without problems on FreeBSD 12.4-RELEASE adm64 with an Intel HD Graphics 500 driver..

Interestingly, deskutils/calibre, which uses also PyQt6, seems to run without problems on FreeBSD 12.4-RELEASE with nvidia drivers. If you're using deskutils/calibre by any chance, can you confirm it too? 


I have not yet had enough time to investigate this further. So far I can say the following:

- Affects only systems with nvidia drivers.
- It seems to be caused by Anki itself in conjunction with PyQt6, because deskutils/calibre, that uses PyQt6 as well, runs in the same environment (= FreeBSD 12.4-RELEASE with nvidia driver) without problems.
- As soon as line #283 in /usr/local/share/anki/aqt/__init__.py is executed by Python, it comes to the core dump.

I'll keep you posted once I've some more info.
